chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: We have seen many good methods of Python strings  But in today's video, I am going to tell you a method of string formatting  Which will surprise you  And you will also appreciate the beauty of Python  fStrings is a method in Python

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Which helps you to place variables in strings very conveniently  Let's go to the computer screen and see how fStrings is made  And we get to see this Python 3.6 version in Python  Such a great feature of Python  Let's see it

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Let's get started  Intro  The first thing I want to tell you about fString is that  It is used for string formatting  fString actually solved a problem

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Which was in Python programming  Python programmers were facing  And that was the problem while formatting a string  Now I will tell you what is string formatting  Suppose I have written here letter is equal to

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: And I made a string here  And I write here, Hey my name is  And after this I put brackets like this  And after doing this I write something like this  And I am very

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Or else, and I am from  And after that I write something like this  Now suppose what happens is  Country  Country

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Is equal to, I write India  And with that I write name is equal to Harry  So I can do something like this  I can say letter.format  And format is a method, a string method

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: And I can say name and country  So if I do something like this  Then the value of name will go in this bracket  And the value of country will go in that bracket  In total what will happen is

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: If I print this  I print this, print letter.format  So what will happen is  I will get my name is Harry  And I am from India

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: As you can see here  So what did it do, format method  Put the name here and put the country here  Now suppose something like this happens  That I would have given something like this

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Country name  Then what would have happened, it would have been reversed  Country first, name later  My name is India and I am from Harry  Which is wrong, so what can I do to fix this

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: I can put a number here  Which is giving my first value after format  I will write 0 here and 1 here  So country will come here and name will come here  So in the first python string formatting was done like this

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: See, hey my name is Harry and I am from India  Now there is no problem with this approach  It is not that you can't use it now  You can definitely do it  And you will also get to see this in old programs

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: The problem is that it does not give convenience  Somewhere I have to write a lot of code  And even after that I have to understand  If it is 0 then country has to be kept here  If it is name then name 1 is here

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Then name has to be kept here  If we give arguments to this format in 5, 10  Then you will keep looking for where  What value of which variable will come  That's why there is an easy way to do it

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: F string  Which is written like this in python  You first write f then make a string  This will automatically recognize python  As an f string

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: F string means that  You can now put variables in your string  So I have written here  Hey my name is  I can write it in such a way

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: That I can put variables directly here  I can put name here as a variable  And what will I put here  I will put country here  So automatically this string

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Python as an f string  Will recognize it first  After that python will say ok  Because you have used f string  And you have written name in curly brackets

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: That's why let me populate the value of name  The value of name which is in this case  Harry, let's populate it here  Populated  And I am from country

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Let me populate the value of country  So India will come here  That's about it  F string is so simple  Now here I have told you about string formatting

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: That you can do it in this way  And do you know what happens by putting point 2f  If I write here price colon point 2f  What will happen  The value of the price

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Let me print it and show you  And if I write 49.099999  Then it will take only two decimal places  So here you can see  It has taken only two decimal places

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: So this kind of work can also be done  If you want to take only two decimal places  Of one floating value  Then you can take  I have put price point 2f here

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: So in this way  You can use f string  Conveniently  Now if I have to write this thing with f string  Then what I would have done simply

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: That I would have made it f string  And after that what I would have done  I wrote txt is equal to this  And it is saying undefined variable price  So I will do one thing

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: I will define the value of price here  By pressing ctrl x  I will comment this out  And if I run it now  So you guys see here

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: For only 49.  Wait a minute  I haven't printed txt  Print txt  Do it

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: So as soon as I print txt  For only 49.10 dollars  Which means the work we used to do with format  Including this point 2f  If I want only two decimal points

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Then I can write colon and space  Point 2f, point 3f  I can do this with f string  So this is the thing in f string  I can do something like this

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: I have put double quotes  I have written my string in it  And I have put my variables in curly braces  I hope you guys understood this thing  Now this is newly introduced

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: So you should know this thing  And you guys can directly insert variables here  Do read it  I have written it  If you have only one variable

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Like in this case if you want to make a string  With value 60 inside of it  You can make it  You can do this kind of work  And you will get this

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: 2x30 value will be there as a string  And  I hope this works  I think there is a bracket missing here  I will fix it

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Oops I have put this bracket in wrong place  So I will fix this thing  So in total 60 will be there  You can see  And if I print its type

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Then definitely it will be a string  Because f string will be a string  I have run it  And you guys can see  Class str is there

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Its class is a string  So this is the thing in f string python  You can use f string  In big strings where we want to plug variables  Just by using curly braces

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: We can insert variables  Inside it  I hope you guys understood  Now one important thing I want to tell you  That if you want to

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Print this thing literally  Literally means  This thing written here  I want to see this thing here  Like here it has replaced value

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Of name  And here it has replaced value of name  So it has been populated  In my string  Now let's say I want to show this

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: In your output  My name is curly braces  Let's say I want to do something like this  We use f strings  Like this

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Let's say I want to show this  I am writing program in between  I want to use f string like this  So I want to see name printed here  If I run it now

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: It will replace value of name  You can see here  My name is Harry and I am from India  We use f string like this  But

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: If I want  As it is display  I will do like this  I will insert curly braces like this  And you can see here

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: If I run it now  You can see it is showing as it is  So if you want  Your curly brackets  To be visible in your string

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: You have to insert double curly brackets  Many people don't know this  They struggle  And you will come to one point of your Python programming career  When you will feel

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Yes this thing is useful  And when you will feel like  F string replaces variable  How do I retain my f string  As an f string

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Like my raw f string  So you can print like this  If you haven't accessed the playlist  Click here to bookmark  And click here to save it

chunks.title: f-strings in Python
chunks.text: Thank you so much guys for watching this video  And I will see you next time
